lineage: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
Valens persecutes the Novatians, because they accepted the Orthodox Faith.

ccel.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

title: Valens persecutes the Novatians, because they accepted the Orthodox Faith.

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: The emperor however did not cease his persecution of those who embraced the doctrine of the homoousion, but drove them away from Constantinople: and as the Novatians acknowledged the same faith, they also were subjected to similar treatment.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: He commanded that their churches should be shut up, also their bishop they sent into exile.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: His name was Agelius, a person that had presided over their churches from the time of Constantine, and had led an apostolic life: for he always walked barefoot, and used but one coat, observing the injunction of the gospel.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: But the emperor’s displeasure against this sect was moderated by the efforts of a pious and eloquent man named Marcian, who had formerly been in military service at the imperial palace, but was at that time a presbyter in the Novatian church, and taught Anastasia and Carosa, the emperor’s daughters, grammar; from the former of whom the public baths yet standing, which Valens erected at Constantinople, were named.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: From respect for this person therefore the Novatian churches which had been for some time closed, were again opened.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: The Arians however would not suffer this people to remain undisturbed, for they disliked them on account of the sympathy and love the Novatians manifested toward the Homoousians, with whom they agreed in sentiment.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Such was the state of affairs at that time.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: We may here remark that the war against the usurper Procopius was terminated about the end of May, in the consulate of Gratian and Dagalaïfus.
